title: DC-DC Charger Architecture
decision: If a future DC source is added, use the Victron Orion XS 1400 as the accepted Orion XS option for 12V/24V source to 8S/24V LiFePO4 DC-DC charging. Do not use the Orion XS 12/12-50A for the 24V battery bank. DC-DC charging is deferred for v1 because the intended charging paths are AC shore, Rivian 120V AC, and solar.
openQuestions: What exact Rivian or external DC source will feed the charger?
What continuous input current may be drawn safely from that source?
Will the charger be controlled by remote on/off, source voltage detection, a BMS allow-to-charge signal, or a service switch?

title: Charging Input Paths
decision: Use a covered 15A 120VAC input feeding a Victron Phoenix Smart IP43 24/25 AC charger, plus an Anderson Powerpole PP45 solar input feeding a Victron SmartSolar MPPT 100/30. Target 2S portable solar at 200W-500W, Vmp 36V-44V, cold-corrected Voc below 75V, and Isc no higher than 15A at the dock input. Skip DC-DC charging in v1.
openQuestions: Confirm exact AC inlet part number.
Confirm exact portable solar panels before final PV fuse/breaker sizing.
Confirm final charger settings after BMS model/settings are selected.

title: BMS Selection and Charge Policy
decision: Use one JK-B2A8S20P per battery module. Operate v1 with one charging source at a time. Use conservative LF105 settings: 3.60V OVP, 2.80V UVP, 75A discharge OCP, 40A charge OCP, 2C charge low-temp cutoff, two cell temperature sensors, and wired service/display access plus Bluetooth.
openQuestions: Recheck BMS settings after first bench test and thermal test.

title: Module Fuse and Disconnect
decision: Use a 60A Littelfuse JLLN060 Class T fuse per module, located close to pack positive, followed by a Blue Sea 6006 manual service disconnect before the SB120 positive. Treat the Blue Sea 6006 as a no-load isolator, not a normal load-breaking switch.
openQuestions: Confirm final fuse holder packaging once the module enclosure layout is drawn.
Confirm exact insulated covers/boots for fuse holder and switch studs.

evidence.label: Littelfuse JLLN Class T fuse datasheet

title: Dock Input Protection
decision: Use one Blue Sea 285-Series 60A DC breaker on each dock module positive input. Keep the internal module 60A Class T fuse; the dock breaker provides dock-side input protection and service isolation but does not replace module-level fault protection.
openQuestions: Confirm exact Blue Sea 285-Series part number once mounting style is chosen.
Confirm breaker placement in the dock physical layout.

title: Dock Busbar, Shunt, and Main Output
decision: Use covered 250A-class positive and negative busbars, a Victron SmartShunt 500A, a 100A Littelfuse JLLN100 Class T main output fuse, and an Anderson SB120 main output wired with 2 AWG fine-strand copper. Reserve future inverter space only; do not install an inverter takeoff in v1.
openQuestions: Confirm exact covered busbar part numbers after stud count and dock layout are drawn.
Confirm exact SB120 housing color/key for the main output.

evidence.label: Littelfuse JLLN Class T fuse datasheet

title: Module Paralleling and Precharge
decision: Add a current-limited precharge/equalization path for each dock module input using a 25 ohm 50W resistor, 2A fuse, and momentary switch. Provide module positive, bus positive, and common negative test points. Target 0.10V or less before paralleling; do not parallel above 0.20V delta.
openQuestions: Confirm exact resistor, fuse holder, and momentary switch part numbers.
Confirm test point style and placement in dock layout.

title: Wire Harness and Cable Fabrication
decision: Use marine-grade tinned fine-strand copper cable with 105C insulation where practical. Build v1 cables in-house using proper crimp tooling, adhesive heat shrink, labels, continuity checks, pull checks, and visual inspection. No cable goes into final assembly unless it passes the cable fabrication acceptance guide.
openQuestions: Confirm exact lug, heat-shrink, and SB contact part numbers.
Confirm final cable lengths after physical layout.

title: Connector Keying Strategy
decision: Use gray Anderson SB120 6810G1 housings with 1319G6 6 AWG contacts for module inputs; red Anderson SB120 6810G3 housings with 1319 2 AWG contacts for main 24V output; SB120 environmental boots/covers 3-6034P1/3-6035P1; PP45 for solar input with MC4 adapter; red/black Powerpole for regulated accessory outputs; Deutsch DTM 4-pin for BMS service/display if the JK harness pinout matches.
openQuestions: Confirm exact low-current accessory connector panel parts after load layout.
Confirm final BMS display/service pinout against the JK harness.

title: Mechanical Enclosure and Retention
decision: Use aluminum module enclosures with nonconductive liners, service panels, guided rails, positive latches, and secondary retention. Design retention for 5g forward, 3g lateral, and 3g vertical. SB120 connectors are not structural.
openQuestions: Confirm final enclosure CAD and fastener pattern.
Confirm exact latch/rail hardware after frunk measurement.

title: Cell Compression and Pack Mechanics
decision: Use a dedicated compression frame with G10/fiberglass insulation, 0.125 in aluminum end plates, and four 1/4-20 insulated threaded rods or equivalent straps. Busbars are electrical only, not structural.
openQuestions: Confirm LF105 terminal torque from purchased cell supplier documentation.
Confirm final compression gap/force after actual cells and hardware are measured.

title: Load Distribution and Accessories
decision: Use a fused 24V branch block for native 24V loads and a Victron Orion-Tr 24/12-20 isolated converter for regulated 12V accessories. Run fridge, Starlink, USB-C PD, lighting, and small electronics from labeled fused branches.
openQuestions: Confirm exact USB-C PD module part numbers.
Confirm exact panel output count after dock layout.
Confirm final ICECO and Starlink cable terminations when cables are purchased.

title: Thermal Design and Validation
decision: Use passive cooling with aluminum heat-spreader mounting for electronics. Validate with staged load and charge tests using IR camera and contact thermocouples. Pass if no hot termination exceeds comparable cable by more than 20C and all components remain within ratings.
openQuestions: Confirm final temperature limits against any purchased-component manuals that are stricter than this baseline.

title: Monitoring, Service, and Future Inverter Boundary
decision: Use SmartShunt 500A monitoring, labeled voltage test points, service checklists, spare fuses, and a label map. Reserve inverter space only in v1; no inverter connector, fuse, or energized branch is installed.
openQuestions: Confirm final label artwork after physical layout.
Confirm whether future inverter target exceeds 100A before adding any inverter hardware.
